leadText: Fairy Feather (Japanese: ようせいのハネ Fairy Feather) is a type of held item introduced in Generation IX that boosts the power of Fairy-type moves.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Effect [ edit | edit source ] Boosts the power of the holder's Fairy-type moves by 20%.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description S V ZA An item to be held by a Pokémon. This feather, which gleams faintly when hit by light, boosts the power of the holder's Fairy-type moves.

sections.title: In the core series games
sections.text: Contents 1 In the core series games 1.1 Price 1.2 Effect 1.3 Description 1.4 Acquisition In the core series games [ edit | edit source ] Price [ edit | edit source ] Games Cost Sell price S V N/A $ 750 ZA $ 4,980 $ 750 Effect [ edit | edit source ] Boosts the power of the holder's Fairy-type moves by 20%. Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description S V ZA An item to be held by a Pokémon. This feather, which gleams faintly when hit by light, boosts the power of the holder's Fairy-type moves. Acquisition [ edit | edit source ] Games Finite methods Repeatable methods S V TM Ogre Oustin' (after completing Normal difficulty for the first time) S V ID Item Printer ZA Reward for completing Side Mission 042: "A Fan of Fairy Types" Racine Construction Shop

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] The Fairy Feather is the last non-consumable type-enhancing item to be introduced that is not related to a specific Pokémon, such as Plates . Prior to the introduction of the Fairy Feather in The Teal Mask , the Fairy Gem and Pixie Plate were introduced as held items that boost the power of Fairy-type attacks, but only the latter has ever been made obtainable.
